SystemName: PvP Combat & World Conflict Bible (Final Canon Version)
Philosophy: PvP exists as a fully integrated extension of the PvE combat, death, loot, and penalty systems with no mechanical divergence or parallel progression tracks.

CanonicalRules.Text: PvP and PvE share identical combat, death, loot, and penalty systems with no mechanical divergence.
Cities and designated safe hubs completely disable all forms of PvP interaction.
All non-safe zones are PvP-enabled and function under full open-world combat conditions.
PvP and PvE deaths are mechanically identical in terms of item loss, corpse generation, and economic consequences.
Blessings mitigate both PvP and PvE death penalties using a unified linear system and are fully consumed upon death.
All player corpses are globally lootable regardless of PvP or PvE origin.
PvP is an economic redistribution system integrated into the global loot and death economy.
PvP exists as an extension of PvE systems, not as a parallel system.

SafeZoneRules.Description: Safe zones include cities and protected hubs. Entering these zones immediately clears aggressive combat flags and disables damage vectors.

BlessingMitigation.ConsumptionLaw: Any death (PvP or PvE) fully consumes all active blessings.

EconomicDrivers.WealthRedistribution: Redistributes equipped templates and carried inventory directly to victorious combatants or scavengers.
EconomicDrivers.RiskAmplifier: Amplifies item circulation risk and serves as a major driver for the high-end template replacement economy.
EconomicDrivers.BlessingSink: Guarantees blessing consumption and continuous coin demand through temple altars.
EconomicDrivers.ScarcityReinforcement: Reinforces overall equipment scarcity, specifically balancing high-end template persistence.
